content.text:
{To everything there is a season, and a time for every matter under heaven.}{Ecclesiastes 3:1}
 {The differential equation is the natural language of change.}{— Morris Kline}

Linear algebra provides the key to solving systems of differential 
equations.

content.title: Solution via Eigenvalues
content.content:
If $A$ is diagonalizable with $A = PDP\inv$, then:
\[
\vect{x}(t) = c_1 e^{\lambda_1 t}\vect{v}_1 + \cdots + c_n e^{\lambda_n t}\vect{v}_n
\]
where $\lambda_i$ are eigenvalues and $\vect{v}_i$ are eigenvectors.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Coupled oscillators
content.solution:
Eigenvalues: $\lambda = -1, -2$. Eigenvectors: $(1, -1)$, $(1, -2)$.
\[
\vect{x}(t) = c_1 e^{-t}\begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ -1 \end{bmatrix} + 
c_2 e^{-2t}\begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ -2 \end{bmatrix}
\]

content.text:
System is **stable** (solutions $\to 0$) iff all eigenvalues have 
negative real parts.

marginNotes.text:
Let there be lights in the expanse of 
the heavens to separate the day from the night.
